---
title: "Knitr and R basics for the course 4EK662 / 4EK462 "
author: " "
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---


# Goals for this script:

* R Markdown - quick review of the `.Rmd` format and functionality
* R basics for the course 4EK662 / 4EK462: outline


---

## R Markdown - quick review 

> This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

For a simple cheatsheet, go to: `RStudio: Help > Cheatsheets > R Markdown Cheat Sheet`  

When you *click* the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

**Target formats**

- html  
- MS Word
- pdf output requires additional SW (MikTeX)


Formatting in Rmd files:  

* Two empty spaces at the end of a line force a line return  
* Lists are insterted using `*`
* Horizontal lines `---`
* Headings are produced using `#`, `##`, etc. 
* Math expression are inserted through $\LaTeX$ syntax: `$\hat{\beta}_1$` translates to $\hat{\beta}_1$, `$\sigma_i^2$` becomes $\sigma_i^2$, etc.



---

# Heading 1
is produced using `# Heading 1`,  

## Heading 2  
is produced using `## Heading 2`, etc.  

### Heading 3  

-----

#### `R` code \& output can be inserted using code "chunks":  

**R-based elements:**

```{r}
library(sf)
#North Carolina (USA) sample dataset from the {sf} package
nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package="sf"))
head(nc)
```


```{r}
summary(nc)
```

Additional information on the data:
```{r, echo=T}
str(nc)
```


You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r, echo=T}
plot(st_geometry(nc))
# plot just the first line/country:
plot(st_geometry(nc[1,]))
```

Alternatively, you can hide the plot command from the output by using `{r, echo=FALSE}`:

```{r, echo=FALSE}
plot(st_geometry(nc))
```

___    
   


## R essentials for the course:

Required/assumed `R` knowledge and skills (and references to self-study materials, if necessary)

1. Basic `R` syntax, working with common objects such as vectors, dataframes, matrices. Referencing styles: `[,]` and `$`. Reading and writing `csv` and similar data formats. Basic linear regression in `R` & statistical inference.  
    + You can use any text or course located on [Stack Overflow for R](https://stackoverflow.com/tags/r/info)  
    
2. Basic knowledge of data plotting using `graphics` and `ggplot2` packages.  
    + [ggplot2 web page](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/)  
    
3. Basic knowledge of `tidyverse`-based data wrangling, with some focus on the `tidyr` and `dplyr` packages.
    + [self-study materials for tidyr](https://tidyr.tidyverse.org/reference/tidyr-package.html)  
    + [self-study materials for dplyr](https://dplyr.tidyverse.org/)  

---
